title: IESET Policy Specification
description: One reform or coherent reform bundle scored on policy axes. Policy-content coding per Invariant 3 — identify what was done, not the party label. The enacted_by list points at movement_ids; axes_moved references axes.yaml ids; linked_hypotheses points at hypotheses the policy's outcome bears on.

status.description: draft=corpus-mined rough capture. candidate=curated. canonical=reviewed, first-class on public platform.

title.description: Human-readable name, e.g. 'Energiewende' or 'Hartz IV labour reform'.

countries.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3. Supranational policies (EU directives, etc) list the affected states.

enacted_date.description: YYYY-MM-DD of primary statute / regulation / executive order.

description.description: One-paragraph plain statement of what the policy actually did. Descriptive, not evaluative.

enacted_by.description: movement_ids (per movements/*.yaml) that enacted this policy. A policy may have multiple — e.g. Obamacare was enacted under Democratic trifecta but modified under Trump.

scope.description: Default 'national' (federal/national legislation). 'subnational' for state/province/municipal-level policies — California climate acts, Quebec referenda, Berlin rent caps, NYC HSTPA, etc. 'supranational' for EU directives, treaty provisions. Optional; absence implies national.

coalition_label_at_enactment.description: Informational — the party/coalition label at time of enactment. Per Invariant 3 this does NOT drive scoring.

axes_moved.description: What axes this policy moved and by how much.

axis.description: Must reference an id defined in axes.yaml. Cross-checked by validate_specs.py.

direction.description: Sign of movement along the axis. '+' / '-' per axes.yaml direction_semantics. '0' means explicitly-confirmed-neutral (measured, no movement). 'mixed' is for bundled reforms whose provisions move the axis in both directions.

magnitude.description: Size of movement. Optional; omit when magnitude is contested.

rationale.description: Short explanation referencing the policy text or outcome measurement.

intended.description: True if the movement's stated goal included this direction on this axis. False flags unintended axis effects (e.g. Energiewende reduced supply security unintentionally).

linked_hypotheses.description: hypothesis_ids whose result this policy's outcome bears on. Human-authored.

linked_hypotheses_inferred.description: Top hypothesis_ids matched to this policy by axis-overlap. Derived by scripts/close_gaps.py; distinct from the human-authored linked_hypotheses.

evaluation_design.description: Optional policy-level measurement design for interventions where the headline statutory change is not enough to identify second-order effects. Used especially for rent controls, price controls, subsidies, and deregulations with explicit exempt categories or nearby comparison markets.

position_predictions.description: What each school of thought predicted or would predict about the outcome of this policy.

outcome_summary.description: One-paragraph plain summary of measured outcomes. Populated after linked_hypotheses have run. Empty until then.

steelman.description: Strongest charitable case for the policy (or against it if the framework-level stance is critical). Path to markdown file.

source_provenance.description: Set by corpus mining for drafts. Points to anchor(s) in private archive; not published.

references.description: External citations — primary legislation, reputable analyses.
